project.name: CAPHR
project.summary: Cluster API infrastructure provider for Hetzner Robot bare-metal servers running Talos Linux, including CRDs, controller logic, docs, examples, and image release workflow.
goals: Provide the Go controller, APIs, CRDs, RBAC, examples, and container image for Hetzner Robot bare-metal Cluster API infrastructure.
Own the Talos-specific provisioning flow that discovers hardware facts, injects them into machine config, and applies config via Talos APIs.
Keep Host ownership, destructive provisioning policy, remediation, generated CRDs, tests, docs, and release artifacts coherent.
nonGoals: Own generic Hetzner Cloud support, kubeadm/Ubuntu flows, cloud-init, application workloads, or product cluster policy outside the provider contract.
Own external storage safety decisions except through the narrow HetznerRobotHostRelease authorization surface documented in this repo.
Own enterprise doctrine or product-specific GitOps environment policy.

owns.name: caphr-provider-contract
owns.description: The Hetzner Robot Cluster API provider APIs, CRDs, RBAC, Go module, controller image, examples, and documented controller contract.

owns.name: talos-bare-metal-provisioning
owns.description: The Talos-specific rescue, hardware fact discovery, config injection, host ownership, destructive provisioning authorization, remediation, and reconciliation behavior implemented by CAPHR.

doesNotOwn: Generic Hetzner Cloud support, kubeadm/Ubuntu installimage flows, cloud-init, application workloads, or workload cluster product policy.
External storage safety, backup, quorum, or Ceph health decisions except through the documented HetznerRobotHostRelease authorization surface.
Enterprise engineering doctrine and manifest schema owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.

publicSurfaces.name: README, architecture docs, comparison docs, and examples

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema and engineering doctrine
Cluster API Go modules and controller-runtime contracts
Talos machinery APIs and machine configuration contract

forbiddenCouplings: Do not add product-specific cluster topology, workload policy, backup policy, or GitOps environment assumptions to the provider core.
Do not bypass the documented Host ownership and destructive provisioning authorization model for convenience.
Do not fork doctrine standards into this repository.

delivery.deployPath: Pull requests and merge queue refs run Go CI with gofmt checks and go test ./...; main and tag pushes build and push the controller image to GHCR and tags create GitHub releases.
delivery.productionProof: Passing CI, generated CRD/RBAC freshness, image digest or GHCR registry readback, and cluster reconciliation or smoke evidence for infrastructure-impacting changes.

gaps.description: CI exists and supports merge_group, but branch-protection required contexts are not recorded in this manifest baseline.
CRD/RBAC/generated-object freshness should be enforced before API or controller contract changes are promoted beyond baseline adoption.
gaps.target: next API or CRD behavior change

gaps.description: Main/tag image publication should record digest, registry readback, and rollback or forward-fix evidence before production lifecycle promotion.
